news.headline: L2 Capital adds 3 industry leaders to Robbins Sports Surfaces board to advance strategic initiatives in sports and performing arts markets.
news.summary: L2 Capital has recently welcomed three new independent board members to Robbins Sports Surfaces, Inc. Barry Hughson, Thomas Trojian, and John Shiver have joined the Board of Directors, showcasing Robbins' dedication to enhancing its presence in the sports and performing arts sectors. With extensive experience in dance, performing arts, and sports, the trio will contribute valuable insights to drive Robbins' strategic goals forward. Their appointments demonstrate Robbins' commitment to excellence and growth in these markets.

news.headline: Intuit (INTU) and Amazon (AMZN) partner to help millions of Amazon sellers manage finances and grow business seamlessly.
news.summary: In Mountain View, Calif., Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ: INTU) and Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) have revealed a long-term strategic collaboration aiming to support numerous Amazon sellers in handling their finances, ensuring compliance, obtaining capital, and expanding their enterprises. Through the utilization of Intuit’s artificial intelligence-driven expert platform, millions of Amazon sellers can effortlessly explore and utilize Intuit’s platform. This integration will enable them to access valuable financial insights such as profitability, cash flow, and estimated tax details.

news.headline: Google achieves quantum computing breakthrough: unveils machine surpassing tasks beyond traditional supercomputers in 10^24 years.
news.summary: Google recently introduced an experimental machine designed to perform tasks that even a conventional supercomputer would take 10 septillion years to complete. This time frame surpasses the age of the universe itself.

news.headline: Global Cloud VPN market was $9.3B in 2023, expected to reach $33.8B by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 20.2% due to remote work surge.

news.headline: Elon Musk criticizes IRS for outdated computers, offers to help improve efficiency as IT consultant.
news.summary: The self-proclaimed US efficiency expert has offered his services as a volunteer IT consultant for the Trump administration.

news.headline: Chinese tech firms, including Alibaba, surge in US trading as China promises more stimulus. Trip.com also rises.
news.summary: On Monday, Alibaba stock surged among a group of Chinese tech companies trading in the U.S. following news of China's commitment to provide additional economic stimulus. The boost in Alibaba's stock was particularly notable, accompanied by an upward trend in Trip.com's stock value. This positive shift in the market came as China promised further stimulus measures, signaling potential growth and investment opportunities in the tech sector. The focus on these companies in the U.S. trading market reflects growing investor optimism, with significant gains seen in Alibaba's stock and Trip.com's performance in response to China's latest economic pledges.

This increase indicates the stock's growing strength in the market. The upgrade is a positive sign for investors, suggesting that Fastly's stock is outperforming a large majority of other stocks. This development underscores the company's potential for growth and success.

Maxey, recognized for his exceptional talent in basketball, will be joining forces with the renowned company known for its remarkable timepieces and exquisite jewelry pieces.

news.headline: Value locked in DeFi approaches levels seen earlier this year due to liquid restaking and Bitcoin Layer 2 solutions.
news.summary: There has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the country, with a total of 10,000 new cases reported in the past week alone. This brings the total number of confirmed cases to 50,000. The spike in cases has put a strain on the healthcare system, with hospitals reaching full capacity in several regions. Authorities are urging the public to follow safety guidelines, such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing, to help curb the spread of the virus. Additionally, vaccination efforts are being ramped up, with a goal of vaccinating 70% of the population by the end of the year.

news.headline: Sonnet BioTherapeutics announces $3.9M offering for 1,085,325 shares & warrants, plus private placement of 673,000 shares & warrants at $2.23.
news.summary: Sonnet BioTherapeutics Holdings, Inc. has announced a definitive agreement with institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 1,085,325 shares of its common stock and warrants to buy up to the same number of shares, in a registered direct offering. The shares are priced at $2.23 each with warrants priced at $2.10 per share, and they will expire in five years. Additionally, a concurrent private placement with an existing investor involves the sale of 673,000 shares of common stock and warrants for the same number of shares at the same prices. This move aims to raise funds for the clinical-stage company developing targeted immunotherapeutic drugs.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency market prices fall; U.S. Bitcoin ETF inflows near $10B. Bitcoin -$97,080.94 (-2.8%), Ethereum -$3,798.59 (-4.7%), Dogecoin -$0.4355 (-6.3%). Large transaction volumes down by 2.4%, 216,675 traders liquidated in 24 hours.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets are on the decline despite U.S. Bitcoin ETF inflows nearing $10 billion. Current prices show Bitcoin at $97,080.94 (-2.8%), Ethereum at $3,798.59 (-4.7%), Solana at $222.86 (-5.6%), Dogecoin at $0.4355 (-6.3%), and Shiba Inu at $0.00002971 (-7.2%).Statistics reveal that large transaction volume has decreased by 2.4%, with daily active addresses increasing by 8.7%. Transactions over $100,000 saw a decrease from 9,621 to 9,044 in a single day. Exchanges netflows have surged by 156.6%. Coinglass data shows that 216,675 traders were liquidated in the past 24 hours, amounting to $567.91 million.In other news, El Salvador has eased Bitcoin regulations to finalize a $1.3 billion deal. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Canada MedLaser expands its Toronto reach with fourth clinic opening in Queen West, offering signature treatments.
news.summary: Canada MedLaser, a reputable provider of cutting-edge skin and body treatments, recently inaugurated its newest facility in Toronto on November 14, 2024. Situated at 8 Gladstone Ave, near Gladstone Hotel in Queen West, this marks the fourth location in Toronto for the esteemed medical aesthetic brand. The clinic aims to provide a wide range of non-surgical treatments tailored to address common aesthetic issues. Specializing in enhancing the appearance of both men and women, the new clinic offers convenient access to innovative solutions for various skin and body concerns.

news.headline: Progressive declares $4.50 annual and $0.10 quarterly dividends on Dec. 6, 2024. Annual meeting record date announced.
news.summary: In Mayfield Village, OH on December 9, 2024, The Progressive Corporation (NYSE: PGR) announced its Board of Directors' decision on December 6, 2024, to declare annual and quarterly dividends for common shares.
